pr.title: Split AXDriver and Synth, Lower MWCC Version
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Split the large audio assembly blob previously grouped under AXDriver by moving roughly 4k lines into a new asm/synth.s object, added synth.o to the build, labeled several audio/synth functions from prior debugging, and changed the project MWCC setting/documentation from compiler version 1.1 to 1.0 as the earliest available approximation. The diff also propagated known SDK symbol names such as OSRestoreInterrupts across many assembly call sites.
what_changed: Updated README compiler setup instructions to place MWCC Build 158 under tools/mwcc_compiler/1.0/ instead of 1.1/.
Created asm/synth.s as a new assembly file of about 3998 lines.
Reduced asm/AXDriver.s by moving most of its earlier contents out, leaving the AXDriver portion and relabeling local labels around AXDriver allocation/free routines.
Added $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/synth.o before AXDriver.o in obj_files.mk so the newly split object is linked as part of TEXT_O_FILES.

Renamed or exposed several audio/synth symbols in assembly and include/functions.h, including HSD_AudioMalloc, HSD_AudioFree, HSD_SynthSFXSampleLoadCallback, HSD_SynthSFXHeaderLoadCallback, HSD_SynthSFXLoadNewProc, HSD_SynthSFXLoad, HSD_SynthInit, AXDriverAlloc, and AXDriverFree.
Replaced many raw func_8034738C branch targets with the identified SDK name OSRestoreInterrupts across AX and other assembly files.
Corrected at least one call site in AXDriver from HSD_AudioMalloc to HSD_AudioFree for freeing the audio allocation, based on the diff excerpt.
smart_moves: Split the monolithic AXDriver assembly by subsystem boundary instead of leaving synth code mixed into AXDriver, making future audio decompilation easier to search and convert incrementally.
Kept object ordering explicit by adding synth.o immediately before AXDriver.o, which preserves link layout expectations while separating source files.
Used prior debugging knowledge to replace anonymous func_ symbols with meaningful HSD_Audio, HSD_Synth, and AXDriver names, improving call graph readability before full C decompilation.
Updated README at the same time as the Makefile compiler version change, avoiding a build setup mismatch for contributors.
Propagated the known OSRestoreInterrupts label across many assembly files, turning repeated raw address calls into a recognizable SDK interrupt pattern.
decomp_lessons: Large assembly blobs can be safely split along recovered subsystem boundaries when object order is preserved in obj_files.mk.
When a function pointer or callback target is identified, naming both the global label and its call/reference sites makes future decomp work substantially easier.
Build-tool version changes should be reflected in both build variables and contributor setup documentation in the same PR.

Recognized SDK functions such as OSRestoreInterrupts should be renamed project-wide once confidence is high, because repeated interrupt-disable/restore idioms are important for reconstructing C control flow.
Audio allocation/free pairs are worth naming early; distinguishing HSD_AudioMalloc from HSD_AudioFree can reveal ownership and lifecycle boundaries in later matching work.
naming_conventions: HSD audio/synth functions use HSD_Audio* and HSD_Synth* prefixes rather than raw func_ addresses when identified.
Synth callback names encode their role, e.g. HSD_SynthSFXSampleLoadCallback and HSD_SynthSFXHeaderLoadCallback.
AX driver-local allocator callbacks were promoted from anonymous labels to AXDriverAlloc and AXDriverFree.
Known Dolphin SDK interrupt routine names use their SDK spelling, e.g. OSRestoreInterrupts.
Unidentified routines remained as func_8038xxxx rather than receiving speculative names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Moved contiguous assembly ranges out of asm/AXDriver.s into asm/synth.s while preserving instruction text and addresses.
Maintained link order by adding synth.o before AXDriver.o, limiting relocation/layout risk from the file split.
Replaced branch targets and address materialization operands with symbol names while preserving the original opcodes and addresses in comments.
Used function naming to document callback relationships, such as references to HSD_SynthSFXSampleLoadCallback and HSD_SynthSFXHeaderLoadCallback.
Changed compiler selection to MWCC_VERSION := 1.0 because the PR body states it was the earliest approximation available.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
The only explicit rationale came from the PR body: the split was based on the author's past debugging, and MWCC was lowered to 1.0 because it was the earliest approximation available.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that convert asm/synth.s functions to C.
Find later references to HSD_SynthSFXLoadNewProc or HSD_SynthInit for refined names/signatures.
Check whether MWCC_VERSION 1.0 remained the default or was changed again in later build-system PRs.
Search for additional func_8034738C aliases that should be OSRestoreInterrupts.
Inspect later audio PRs for struct definitions around HSD_AudioMalloc, HSD_AudioFree, and AXDriverAlloc.
